REV 01: revise the existing open building, plumbing, and HVAC permit at the property. Our building inspector requested updated drawings to reflect structural changes to the foundation and ground floor that were necessitated due to access constraints and existing fire damage uncovered during demolition. pdn.desc: Proposed Zoning By-law amendment re-submission to allow the redevelopment of the site with a 40-storey mixed-use building with a total height of 132.5 metres. The application proposes commercial uses within the ground floor and 459 residential units above. The existing heritage buildings at 241 Richmond St W and 133 John St are proposed to be retained and integrated into the development.  Site access is proposed off Nelson St.

Site Plan application to support the Council approved redevelopment of the site with a 41-storey mixed-use building with a total height of 132.5 metres. The application includes commercial uses within the ground floor and 479 residential units above. The existing heritage buildings at 241 Richmond St W and 133 John St will be retained and integrated into the development.  Site access is off Nelson St.

Zoning By-law Amendment for the development of a 10-storey mixed-use building including a total of 280 dwelling units, 743 square metres of non-residential gross floor area and 141 parking spaces within a two-level underground garage. The proposal includes a total gross floor area of 20,818 square metres, resulting in a density of 4.96 times the area of the lot.

Application to Amend the Zoning By-law to permit the redevelopment of the site at 555 Richmond Street West.  The existing 12-storey office building fronting Richmond Street will be retained and a new 16-storey residential rental building with retail uses on the ground floor fronting Adelaide Street West is proposed. This development proposal comprises Phase 2 of a larger master-planned development which also includes the sites at 141 - 161 Bathurst Street and 579-591 Richmond Street West (Phase 1). The phase 1 and phase 2 development proposals are being reviewed concurrently. Please see Application No. 21- 172654 STE 10 OZ.

This application is being reviewing in conjunction with an application to Amend the Zoning By-law, File No. 21-172654 STE 10 OZ.

Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a mixed-use development including two buildings, 37 and 42 storeys in height (131.0 metres and 145.2 metres, respectively, including mechanical penthouses). Proposed uses include two levels of retail, seven levels of office space, a daycare, the replacement of the existing movie theatre, an on-site publicly accessible privately owned open space and 693 residential units. The total gross floor area proposed is 80,334m2, consisting of 49,983m2 of residential floor area, and 30,351m2 of non-residential floor area.  Two levels of below grade parking are proposed with 231 vehicle and 829 bicycle parking spaces. (Enacted as by-law 949-2022. Third-party appeal received.)

Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a mixed-use development including two buildings, 37 and 42 storeys in height (131.0 metres and 145.2 metres, respectively, including mechanical penthouses). Proposed uses include two levels of retail, seven levels of office space, a daycare, the replacement of the existing movie theatre, an on-site publicly accessible privately owned open space and 693 residential units. The total gross floor area proposed is 80,334m2, consisting of 49,983m2 of residential floor area, and 30,351m2 of non-residential floor area.  Two levels of below grade parking are proposed with 231 vehicle and 829 bicycle parking spaces. (Enacted as by-law 949-2022. Third-party appeal received.)

A re-zoning application and a site plan approval application have been submitted to develop the vacant site with a 4-storey residential building with a total of 94 units.  A total residential gross floor area of 7677m2 is proposed, including indoor amenity areas totalling 1,377 m2. Extensive landscaping and an outdoor amenity space of 520 m2 are proposed around the building. Site vehicular access is proposed from Chapman Road (with existing accesses from Royal York Road and Renault Crescent to be removed), and the proposal includes a single level of underground parking with 50 parking spaces for visitors and employees. A total of 8 short-term bicycle parking spaces are also proposed at-grade at the building entrance.  The building would be geared toward senior tenants as an assisted living/memory care facility.
